title: Astrophotography Workshop: Galleta Meadows
description: Capture the Cosmos in Borrego Springs   Unlock the secrets of astrophotography and learn how to capture stunning Milky Way shots in just one session! Join us just outside the Park in the breathtaking Galleta Meadows and discover the essentials of night sky photography, from planning to execution, in one of the best dark sky spots in the country.          What to Expect:       Hands-on astrophotography guidance in an iconic dark sky location         Step-by-step tips on camera settings, composition, and photo planning         Access to unique, easy-to-reach spots perfect for Milky Way shots         Personalized, small-group instruction to ensure individual attention         Minimal walking—just under 1/10 of a mile from parking to photo locations          Who Should Attend:       Beginners with a basic understanding of camera settings         Anyone eager to capture the beauty of the night sky         Photography enthusiasts looking to improve their astrophotography skills          What to Bring:       DSLR or mirrorless camera         Tripod and lens suitable for night photography         Sturdy closed-toe shoes         Head lamp          Get ready to elevate your photography game under the stars!        REGISTER TODAY!       Date: Friday, October 24, 2025     Time: 6:30 p.m.-8:30 p.m.     Location: Meet at the ABF office at 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #122 Borrego Springs, CA 92004 (in The Mall next to Kendall’s Café). We’ll then carpool/caravan to Galleta Meadows.          Cost:     ABF Member: $60     Non-Member: $70     Student: $35     Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.           What Previous Attendees Are Saying:     “Chris was a great instructor. He really set a great tone at the beginning of the workshop, and he adapted his approach to each student based on their needs. It was a really great workshop! I walked away learning a lot.”     “Great experience for my three-generation family”          About the Instructor: Chris Olivas is an award-winning landscape photographer and professional videographer. He was born and raised in San Diego, California. His education and photography have taken him all over the world. He also has been a science educator for over 14 years. He has an extensive background in astronomy and photography and has won numerous awards for his photography work. He loves the combination of science and art that astrophotography entails and loves to spread his passion for the night sky. He specializes in photographing the Milky Way in adventurous locations and sharing his experiences through educational workshops.
location: 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #122, Borrego Springs, CA 92004 and Galleta Meadows

title: North County Nature Paddle Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us on one of our North County Kayaking adventures! Discover the natural beauty of Agua Hedionda Lagoon on a guided kayaking tour through this unique coastal ecosystem in Carlsbad. As you paddle through calm waters near the lagoon’s wetlands, keep an eye out for snowy egrets, great egrets, and great blue herons foraging along the shoreline. You may also spot a belted kingfisher darting across the water, killdeer along the muddy upland edges, or red-tailed hawks perched high on the powerlines above. Along the way, learn about local conservation efforts, wetland restoration, and the rich biodiversity that makes this lagoon such an important habitat. This eco tour is suitable for all experience levels and offers a meaningful way to connect with nature while supporting environmental education and stewardship. What to Expect: Wildlife Viewing: Watch for native birds and other wildlife as you explore the lagoon by kayak. Educational Insights: Learn about lagoon ecology, restoration projects, and sustainable aquaculture. Birding Support: Binoculars and bird identification guides are provided to enhance your experience. Guided Exploration: Enjoy expert-led interpretation while paddling through this peaceful marine habitat. Join us for an inspiring on-the-water experience highlighting the importance of preserving San Diego’s coastal resources. Group Discounts available on 6 or more tickets. Email eco-tours@oceanconnectors.org
location: California Watersports at Agua Hedionda Lagoon, 4215 Harrison St, Carlsbad, CA

title: 🤖Robot Drivers: FTC - Your First Coding Mission
description:
**This Event is tailored to students in grades 7-12**

What if your child could tell a robot exactly what to do, and watch it follow their commands? In this hands-on introductory workshop, students will step into the world of robotics and programming using FTC SIM, a free game-based simulator developed by FIRST Robotics Canada.
What They'll Learn: Students will control a virtual robot using keyboard commands and program it with drag-and-drop block coding to move, turn, and navigate through challenges. No prior coding experience needed, just curiosity and a willingness to problem-solve. By the end of the hour, every student will have completed their first robot mission.
Why This Matters: Learning to code builds more than tech skills. It strengthens logical thinking, persistence, and creativity. Students learn to break big problems into smaller steps, test their ideas, and iterate when things don't work the first time, skills that transfer far beyond the screen.
What to Expect:

* Guided instruction with time for hands-on exploration
* A fun puzzle challenge to apply what they've learned
* A supportive environment where mistakes are part of the process

What to Bring: Just your student! We'll provide computers. You are welcome to bring your computer as well.
Learn more about FTC SIM: [https://ftcsim.org](https://ftcsim.org)
location: 12625 High Bluff Dr. #113, San Diego, CA, 92130

title: Farm Film and Music Series – Tending Nature
description: Summertime Film on the Farm Join us for the July Farm Film & Music where we'll screen Tending Nature: Indigenous Land Stewardship . Tending Nature is a documentary series that explores how, for a thousand years, land has provided for Native peoples and how tribal groups have actively shaped and tended (cared for) the land. Through in-person interviews, the series highlights current projects that are trying to solve ongoing environmental problems by integrating traditional knowledge from tribal groups with Western science. Bring a picnic dinner, your low-back chairs, and your friends and family for an inspiring evening on the Farm with live music, community partners, a panel conversation, and a special outdoor screening under the stars.   Event Details 📅 Date: Thursday, July 23 🕡 6 PM – Gates Open: Enjoy live music from Nathan and Jessie , community partners, and a beautiful Farm setting 🎙️ 7:30 PM - Panel discussion 🎬 8:05 PM –Film Screening Tickets $12/person in advance, $18/person at the door $36/person for Farm Friend Proceeds help support the Farm’s mission to nourish our community and educate about environmental sustainability. Learn more about our impact here . Tending Nature—Indigenous Land Stewardship This "Tending Nature" special features multiple perspectives and voices from Indigenous communities across California who are striving to keep the practices of their heritage alive. From coming-of-age rituals, seasonal food harvests, basket weaving and jewelry making, the documentary shares how traditional practices can be protected and maintained as a way of life for future generations. Hear from Expert Panelists: True Allard , Moderator Member of the American Indian Student Association at CSUSM and Farm employee Shalena Critchlow Indigenous advocate, community organizer, and descendant of the Rincon Band of Luiseño Indians and the Iipay Nation of Santa Ysabel. Representing Tribal Sacred Waters, Inc. Amanda Subish Red Earth Movement Connect with Partner Organizations: Before the start of the film, visit and learn from tabling organizations that share in the mission of nourishing community, responsibly stewarding the land, and more. Tabling Partners: California Native Plant Society Native Plants 4 Encinitas Produce Good Sustainability is Sexy Tribal Sacred Waters, Inc. Wild Ones San Diego   Part of Our Farm Film & Music Series This event opens Coastal Roots Farm’s 2026 Farm Film & Music Series, a seasonal community experience featuring documentary films, live local music, and meaningful conversation on the Farm. Each event invites guests to explore pressing environmental and social issues while gathering in community, enjoying live music, and connecting with local partners. By beginning with Groundswell , this year’s series starts with a message at the heart of the Farm: healthy soil, resilient communities, and collective action can change what is possible. View the full 2026 series lineup here . Thank You to our Event Sponsors Series Title Sponsor: Presenting Sponsor: Interested in supporting this powerful community series? Learn more about sponsorship opportunities here or email Sara@coastalrootsfarm.org .

title: Compost & Mulch Giveaway – 7/25 at Sun Runner Lot
partner_name: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost! This compost is available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours. To increase opportunities for residents to pick up compost, I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of San Diego are partnering to host Compost Giveaways on select weekends. Get your hands on some nutrient-rich compost for your garden at no cost. Whether you're a seasoned gardener or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone looking to give their plants a boost. Swing by and grab some compost to help your garden thrive! Mulch is also available at this event. Things to bring: Shovel Gloves Containers Tarp and bungees for truck beds (ALL TRUCK BEDS MUST BE COVERED!) Things to know: Be prepared to self load compost and mulch All participants are REQUIRED to sign a release waiver Must approach from the EAST Exact Coordinates: (32.76726972788742, -117.20799017856716) Please be aware, in order to collect compost you are REQUIRED to sign a waiver. All participants will need to sign a waiver upon entering the event. About the Miramar Greenery: At the Miramar Greenery, compost is made from yard trimmings and food scraps that have been ground, placed in windrows, turned and watered for 70 days. During this time, microorganisms digest the carbon and nitrogen rich mixture, causing the windrow to sustain temperatures of 140-165 F. This process eliminates most weed seeds and pathogens while breaking down the organic material into beneficial soil nutrients. Finished compost is screened to a particle size of one-half inch or less which also removes film plastic from the final product. For more information, visit https://www.sandiego.gov/environmental-services/miramar Practical Application and Benefits : Compost can be used as an amendment to improve soil texture and increase nutrient and water holding capacity. In San Diego, where soil lacks many essential plant nutrients, mixing compost in with the soil is one of the best things you can do for your yard and garden. Note: This event will occur rain or shine, however if severe weather occurs the days leading up to the event, the event will be rescheduled or canceled. If needed, registrants will receive electronic communication as soon as possible before the event. This event is made possible by the City of San Diego Environmental Services Department and facilitated by I Love A Clean San Diego.
location: Sun Runner Lot, Sea World Dr & Pacific Hwy, San Diego, CA

title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special. Along the way, you may spot osprey diving for fish, great blue herons and egrets wading in the shallows, nesting terns (April to September), and a variety of migrating birds (October to March). With a little luck, guests can even catch a glimpse of a Pacific green sea turtle cruising through the bay! This tour is welcoming to all skill levels and moves at a comfortable pace, with plenty of time to enjoy the surroundings. The route is about two hours long, and wind conditions can vary, adding a bit of adventure while still remaining accessible for beginners. What to Expect: Wildlife Viewing: Paddle through the waters, keeping an eye out for local wildlife, from native birds to coastal critters. Educational Insights: Our expert guides will provide fascinating information about the local ecosystems and conservation efforts. Chula Vista Wetlands: Paddle toward the restored wetlands, a vital area for wildlife and an important conservation success story. Come prepared for a fun and challenging adventure while experiencing the beauty of the Chula Vista Wetlands. Sign up for our next Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour today! Best of all, every tour supports our education programs for South Bay youth! Find more information at www.oceanconnectors.org Group Discounts available on 6 or more tickets. Email eco-tours@oceanconnectors.org
location: Bayside Park, 999 Bayside Pkwy, Chula Vista

description: Venture into the desert after dark for a family-friendly night hike through Coyote Canyon’s Ocotillo Flat! We’ll watch the desert transform after sunset and explore the sights and sounds that make this landscape come alive under the stars.      What to Expect:      We’ll begin our adventure at Ocotillo Flats, surrounded by the silhouettes of ocotillo and creosote under the rising moon. We’ll hike roughly 3 miles roundtrip across gentle (though uneven) terrain.       Along the trail, we’ll look for nocturnal creatures that thrive after dark, such as scorpions.       This easy-paced night hike is led by an ABF staff member and certified Wilderness First Responder, ensuring a safe and fun experience for all ages.      Who Should Attend:      Families and friends looking for a unique way to enjoy summer in the desert.       Nature lovers eager to experience the desert at night and learn about its nocturnal life.       Hikers comfortable with a relatively easy 3-mile walk on sandy, uneven terrain.              REGISTER TODAY!            Date: Saturday, July 25, 2026     Time: 7:00 p.m.-10:00 p.m. (end time is approximate)     Location: Meet at the ABF store at 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92004 (in The Mall next to Calico’s). From there we’ll carpool/caravan to the trailhead.          Cost:     ABF Member: $28     Non-Member: $33     Student: $17     Cancellation Policy: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.           What Past Participants Are Saying:      “Great guides. Fun and informative. It wasn’t something I would have done on my own.”      “Well organized. Educated and educating guides.”      “I appreciated the opportunity to be outdoors and experience the desert in a different way via a night hike.”      “It was a perfect evening under the stars”              Hike Leader:      Born and raised between Thermal and the San Fernando Valley in California, Stacy Valdez’s journey is characterized by a blend of academic achievements, professional roles, and a profound dedication to social causes. Stacy has built a diverse and impactful career with a strong foundation in education and a commitment to community engagement and service. They have been involved in humanitarian efforts through Border Kindness since 2018, reflecting their passion for the outdoors, advocacy, language, and cultural studies. Their love for the environment began at an early age, growing once she moved to the San Fernando Valley, where she identified her love for the mountains and hiking and camping. Stacy is dedicated to promoting the outdoors to underserved youth and their communities.
location: 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92004

title: The A, Bee, Cs of California’s Native Bees with National Geographic Explorer, Krystle Hickman (Webinar)
description: Join National Geographic Explorer Krystle Hickman for this presentation on native bees, their relationships with native plants, and community science engagement.      What to Expect:      Learn how California is home to over 1,600 species of native bees and how they are a vital part of the ecosystem.       Get to know more about these important pollinators through Krystle's stunning macro photography.       Discover what makes them special and how to support a healthy garden.          Who Should Attend:      Home gardeners and native plant enthusiasts interested in supporting pollinators        Nature lovers, photographers, and anyone curious about California’s native bees        Teachers, students, and environmental educators        Community science volunteers and conservation-minded residents        Beekeepers, master gardeners, and habitat restoration volunteers          REGISTER TODAY!        Date: Wednesday, July 29, 2026     Time: 12:00 p.m.–1:00 p.m.      Location: Online via Zoom. Instructions to access the webinar will be sent the week of the event.          Cost:       ABF Member: FREE     Not a member? Join today for as little as $35 and save!     Non-Member: $10      Student: $5      Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.            What Past Participants Are Saying:     “Depth, thoroughness and clarity of information as well as intriguing videos”     “I always eagerly anticipate upcoming virtual programs…they are terrific.”      “The presenter was articulate, well-paced, and really knew the topic.”         Bio:     Krystle Hickman is a National Geographic Explorer, community scientist, conservation photographer, and multi-award-winning author based in Los Angeles, California. Through her photography, she raises awareness about the decline of native bee species while highlighting the ecosystems they reside in.      Hickman’s work has taken her across the globe, where she documents native bees without using lethal collection methods. This approach has enabled her to document behaviors new to science and capture some of the first images of living representatives of many species.      Her work has been featured across television, digital platforms, and podcasts, including Ologies, and she has presented internationally, including at the 2024 United Nations Biodiversity Conference (COP16) in Colombia. Hickman has also lectured at institutions such as Harvard University, UCLA, and more.

title: Anza-Borrego Under the Stars
description: Join Us for a Stargazing Adventure in Anza-Borrego!  Experience the night sky like never before at Anza-Borrego Desert State Park—an official International Dark Sky Park. Explore stars, planets, and constellations through telescopes guided by an astronomer.  What to expect:   A Stunning Night Sky: Experience the awe of a star-filled sky in one of California’s premier Dark Sky Parks—perfect for spotting constellations, planets, and even the Milky Way.    Guided Stargazing: Learn the basics of stargazing, including how to identify major stars, constellations, and planets visible this time of year – with and without a telescope.    Relaxed Atmosphere: Bring your camp chair, snacks, and beverages. You're welcome to hang out, ask questions, and simply enjoy the desert night.    Optional Camping: Extend your visit by camping nearby (this portion is not hosted).   Who should attend:   Nature lovers, astronomy enthusiasts, and anyone curious about the night sky who wants to experience stargazing in a world-class dark-sky location.    Anyone interested in learning how to identify stars, planets, and constellations with guidance from an astronomer. About Coastal Roots Farm We're nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. title: Compost and Mulch Giveaway – 8/15 at Los Penasquitos Ranch House
partner_name: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost and mulch! These products available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours. To increase opportunities for residents to pick up compost, I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of San Diego are partnering to host Compost and Mulch Giveaways on select weekends. Get your hands on some nutrient-rich compost for your garden at no cost. Whether you're a seasoned gardener or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone looking to give their plants a boost. Swing by and grab some compost to help your garden thrive! Things to bring: Shovel Gloves Containers Tarp and bungees for truck beds (ALL TRUCK BEDS MUST BE COVERED!) Things to know: Be prepared to self load compost All participants are REQUIRED to sign a release waiver Must enter from the East (Black Mountain Road) Please be aware, in order to collect compost you are REQUIRED to sign a waiver. All participants will need to sign a waiver upon entering the event. About the Miramar Greenery: At the Miramar Greenery, compost is made from yard trimmings and food scraps that have been ground, placed in windrows, turned and watered for 70 days. During this time, microorganisms digest the carbon and nitrogen rich mixture, causing the windrow to sustain temperatures of 140-165 F. This process eliminates most weed seeds and pathogens while breaking down the organic material into beneficial soil nutrients. Finished compost is screened to a particle size of one-half inch or less which also removes film plastic from the final product. For more information, visit https://www.sandiego.gov/environmental-services/miramar Practical Application and Benefits : Compost can be used as an amendment to improve soil texture and increase nutrient and water holding capacity. In San Diego, where soil lacks many essential plant nutrients, mixing compost in with the soil is one of the best things you can do for your yard and garden. The County of San Diego's Rancho Los Penasquitos - In 1823, Francisco Maria Ruiz built a modest adobe that established Rancho Los Penasquitos as San Diego County's first Mexican land grant, remnants of which still stand within the present-day ranch house. Today, the surrounding park offers more than 10 miles of multi-use trails, a staging area, restrooms, and hosts weddings, reunions, and community events. The site also features the Los Peñasquitos Community Garden, founded in 2012, which provides 20 plots for local residents to grow their own produce through seasonal leases. The historic adobe serves as a cultural landmark and museum, offering visitors guided tours, educational programs, and a glimpse into the region’s early ranching and adobe life. For more information, visit https://www.sdparks.org/content/sdparks/en/park-pages/RanchoLosPenasquitos.html Note: This event will occur rain or shine, however if severe weather occurs the days leading up to the event, the event will be rescheduled or canceled. If needed, registrants will receive electronic communication as soon as possible before the event. This event is made possible by the City of San Diego Environmental Services Department and facilitated by I Love A Clean San Diego.
location: Los Penasquitos Ranch House, 12122 Canyonside Park Drive, San Diego

Celebrate World Lizard Day with us and explore the fascinating world of lizards and all things reptiles on exhibit. Don’t forget to keep your eyes peeled as you stroll our trails and gardens—you might just spot some wild lizard friends soaking up the sun, too! description: Join us for a peaceful moonlit hike through the desert, where wide-open skies, rugged terrain, and the quiet beauty of the landscape come alive after dark.  What to Expect:   Under the glow of the moon and stars, we’ll experience the sights and sounds of the desert at night—from shifting shadows across the landscape to the subtle movement of nocturnal wildlife.    We’ll hike approximately 2-3 miles roundtrip across natural terrain.    Along the trail, you might see nocturnal creatures that thrive after dark, such as scorpions, bats, and owls.    This easy-paced night hike will be led by a wilderness first responder, ensuring a safe and fun experience for all ages.   Who Should Attend:   Nature lovers interested in experiencing the desert in a new and unique way.    Stargazers and those who appreciate wide, dark skies.    Hikers in moderate physical condition, comfortable walking several miles over sand or dirt trails.    Anyone looking for a memorable outdoor adventure at night.      REGISTER TODAY!   Date: Saturday, September 26, 2026  Time: 6:00 p.m.-9:00 p.m. (start and end times are approximate and will be confirmed)  Location: Meet at the ABF store at 587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92004 (in The Mall next to Calico’s). From there we’ll carpool/caravan to the trailhead (location to be determined).    Cost:  ABF Member: $28 Not a member? Join today for as little as $35 and save!  Non-Member: $33  Student: $17  Cancellation Policy: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.     What Past Participants Are Saying:   “Well organized. Educated and educating guides.”   “I appreciated the opportunity to be outdoors and experience the desert in a different way via a night hike.”   “It was a perfect evening under the stars”     Hike Leader:  Matt Nagy is a San Diego native who had the unique experience of growing up across the world. Before graduating high school, he lived in five different countries, including the USA. His global travels have given him the opportunity to see much of the planet, but without a doubt, San Diego County (especially Anza Borrego) remains his favorite place. In his free time, Matt enjoys long hikes, bike rides, and live music. When he has a few weeks off, he embarks on extended adventures such as river trips, bikepacking, or backpacking. Having spent many months under the stars, Matt is always planning his next adventure. He is excited to explore and appreciate the wonders of Anza Borrego with others, creating unforgettable memories along the way.              Don't forget a night sky guide before you head out!

title: Compost & Mulch Giveaway – 10/3 at Sun Runner Lot
partner_name: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost! This compost is available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours. To increase opportunities for residents to pick up compost, I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of San Diego are partnering to host Compost Giveaways on select weekends. Get your hands on some nutrient-rich compost for your garden at no cost. Whether you're a seasoned gardener or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone looking to give their plants a boost. Swing by and grab some compost to help your garden thrive! Mulch is also available at this event. Things to bring: Shovel Gloves Containers Tarp and bungees for truck beds (ALL TRUCK BEDS MUST BE COVERED!) Things to know: Be prepared to self load compost and mulch All participants are REQUIRED to sign a release waiver Must approach from the EAST Exact Coordinates: (32.76726972788742, -117.20799017856716) Please be aware, in order to collect compost you are REQUIRED to sign a waiver. All participants will need to sign a waiver upon entering the event. About the Miramar Greenery: At the Miramar Greenery, compost is made from yard trimmings and food scraps that have been ground, placed in windrows, turned and watered for 70 days. During this time, microorganisms digest the carbon and nitrogen rich mixture, causing the windrow to sustain temperatures of 140-165 F. This process eliminates most weed seeds and pathogens while breaking down the organic material into beneficial soil nutrients. Finished compost is screened to a particle size of one-half inch or less which also removes film plastic from the final product. For more information, visit https://www.sandiego.gov/environmental-services/miramar Practical Application and Benefits : Compost can be used as an amendment to improve soil texture and increase nutrient and water holding capacity. In San Diego, where soil lacks many essential plant nutrients, mixing compost in with the soil is one of the best things you can do for your yard and garden. Note: This event will occur rain or shine, however if severe weather occurs the days leading up to the event, the event will be rescheduled or canceled. If needed, registrants will receive electronic communication as soon as possible before the event. This event is made possible by the City of San Diego Environmental Services Department and facilitated by I Love A Clean San Diego.

Today, the surrounding park offers more than 10 miles of multi-use trails, a staging area, restrooms, and hosts weddings, reunions, and community events. The site also features the Los Peñasquitos Community Garden, founded in 2012, which provides 20 plots for local residents to grow their own produce through seasonal leases. title: Intermediate Hike: Domelands
description: Journey through the sculpted wonders of Anza-Borrego  Explore the otherworldly terrain of the Domelands on this intermediate-level hike through Anza-Borrego Desert State Park. Discover wind and water-carved formations, slot canyons, and sweeping desert views that make this area a true geological treasure.  What to Expect:   Approximately 7 miles of moderate hiking with ~1,200 ft elevation gain, some uneven terrain, off-trail sections and light scrambling    Dramatic badlands, natural arches, and sculpted sandstone domes    Panoramic views of the Carrizo Badlands and Imperial Valley    A chance to see desert wildlife and seasonal blooms    Interpretive insights into the area’s unique geology and desert ecology   Who Should Attend:   Hikers with some experience looking for a moderate challenge    Nature and geology enthusiasts eager to explore off-the-beaten-path areas    Visitors eager to experience one of Anza-Borrego’s most fascinating natural features     REGISTER TODAY!   Date: Saturday, November 14, 2026  Time: 7:00 a.m.-2:00 p.m.  Location: Meet at the trailhead off of S-22. Precise meeting location will be sent before the event.    Cost:  ABF Member: $30  Non-Member: $35  Student: $18    Cancellation Policy & Scholarships: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.  What Previous Attendees Are Saying about Our Hikes:  “I loved the hike choice and challenge. I will definitely be on another hike with the foundation soon!”  “I so enjoyed getting "off trail" and exploring with the Anza Borrego Foundation. The camaraderie, physical challenge, and education around Anza Borrego was beyond my expectations.”  “Expertise was great. cohesiveness of group was good. location excellent.”  Hike Leader:  In his professional life, Sergey Kuhsch has spent over 16 years in software development across different industries. In contrast, much of his free time is dedicated to exploring the natural world. As much as he enjoys hiking in chaparral or exploring the local mountains, he feels that Anza Borrego is a very special place. The sheer size and accessibility of the park are unlike any other in the region. The rugged landscape, endless vistas, and hidden oases offer unique opportunities to explore. Sergey feels that supporting the Anza Borrego Foundation is the most impactful way to support Anza Borrego Desert State Park.                                              You’re invited to explore the scientific detective story in San Diego’s diverse mountains and deserts spanning over 200 million years.  This includes the expansive landscapes of Anza-Borrego Desert State Park which is rich with ancient geological wonders.
location: Anza-Borrego Desert State Park, trailhead off S-22, Borrego Springs

title: Desert Trails: A Primitive Camping Adventure
description: Explore, Hike, and Camp Under the Stars in the Heart of the Desert  Get off the beaten path and discover the rugged beauty of the desert! This two-day adventure combines a scenic driving tour, invigorating hikes, and hands-on primitive camping skills for an unforgettable outdoor experience.  What to Expect:   Guided 4WD/AWD driving tour through Anza-Borrego Desert State Park    Two moderate-level hikes through stunning desert terrain    Learn primitive camping techniques—from setting up camp to outdoor cooking (we’ll provide a list of what to bring!)    Camp under a blanket of desert stars    Connect with fellow outdoor enthusiasts and nature lovers   Who Should Attend:   Adventurers eager to try primitive camping for the first time    Hikers and off-road explorers seeking a new challenge    Anyone who loves the desert and wants to learn wilderness basics     REGISTER TODAY!   Date/Time: 9:00 a.m. Saturday, December 12 – 12:00 p.m. Sunday, December 13, 2026    Location: Meet at the ABF Store (587 Palm Canyon Dr. #110, Borrego Springs, CA 92204). We’ll then carpool/caravan to the start of our driving tour.    Cost:  ABF Member: $102/person Not a member? Join today for as little as $35 and save!  Non-Member: $120/person  Student: $60/person    Cancellation & Scholarship Policy: Please see our FAQs page to read our cancellation policy and learn about scholarship options.      Tour Leaders:  Todd Linke is a resident of San Diego and just finished his 25th year in education, 23 of them with the Grossmont Union High School District. Todd grew up in Ohio and has been exploring, hiking and camping since he was a child. His earliest outdoor memories include hiking in the caves in Hocking Hills, sailing on Lake Atwood, swimming in Lake Erie, and camping at the seaside at Myrtle Beach.  Todd fell in love with the desert several years ago and makes the 2-hour drive to the park every chance he gets. He has visited the park over 100 times and has hiked and driven many of its well-known and more obscure trails and roads, but as always discovering more. Todd brings his love for science and his passion for the desert to any excursion in the park.       In his professional life, Sergey Kuhsch has spent over 16 years in software development across different industries. In contrast, much of his free time is dedicated to exploring the natural world. As much as he enjoys hiking in chaparral or exploring the local mountains, he feels that Anza Borrego is a very special place. The sheer size and accessibility of the park are unlike any other in the region. The rugged landscape, endless vistas, and hidden oases offer unique opportunities to explore. Sergey feels that supporting the Anza Borrego Foundation is the most impactful way to support AnzaBorrego Desert State Park.
